questions.question: Assume you take 10 different random samples of data for a binary classification problem. What is the likely outcome of these samples in terms of the training and testing data for discriminant analysis?
questions.hint: Consider the implications of random sampling on the training and testing datasets

questions.question: Suppose you take 10 different random samples of data for a multiclass classification problem. What is the appropriate way to combine the covariance matrices of these samples to obtain an estimate of the population covariance matrix?
questions.hint: Consider the properties of covariance matrices for multiclass classification

questions.question: You take 10 different random samples of data for a generalized linear model. What is the expected impact on the model's performance if the samples are taken from different classes in the data?
questions.hint: Consider the implications of class imbalance on model performance
